recalls.Description: Dynabook Americas is recalling approximately 15.5 million Toshiba laptop AC adapters due to potential overheating and sparking, which can lead to burn and fire hazards. These adapters were sold with Toshiba laptops and separately from April 2008 to April 2014. The affected models have date codes ranging from April 2008 to December 2012, identifiable by specific model and serial numbers printed on the rating label.
recalls.Title: Dynabook Americas Issues Recall for 15.5 Million Toshiba Laptop AC Adapters Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Dynabook at 800-457-7777 from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email dba-acadapter2024@dynabook.com, or visit https://support.dynabook.com/recall2024ax for more details.

Products.Name: Toshiba Laptop AC Adapters
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 15.5 million (In addition, about 1.3 million were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Toshiba AC Adapter with model name/number and serial number location
Recalled Toshiba AC Adapter with model name/number and serial number location
Recalled Toshiba AC Adapter with model name/number and serial number location
Recalled Toshiba AC Adapter with model name/number for PA-1750-04 and serial number location

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 679 reports of the recalled AC adapters overheating or catching on fire, melting, and burning. This includes 43 reports of minor burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at various retailers nationwide and on Toshiba.com from April 2008 through April 2014, sold either with laptops or separately for $25 to $75.

Importers.Name: Dynabook Americas Inc., of Irvine, California (importer at the time of sale was Toshiba America Information Systems, Inc.)

Hazards.Name: Overheating and sparking, leading to burn and fire hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled adapters immediately and reach out to Dynabook for a free replacement. Instructions for identifying affected adapters and ordering replacements can be found at https://support.dynabook.com/axx2024. A photo of the adapter with the power cord cut must be submitted to dba-acadapter2024@dynabook.com to receive the replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: Dynabook recalls 15.5 million Toshiba laptop AC adapters due to fire and burn hazards. Contact for a free replacement.

recalls.Description:
CableMod is recalling its 12VHPWR Angled Adapters, specifically models V1.0 and V1.1, due to potential fire and burn hazards. These adapters connect to a graphics processing unit's power port at either a 90-degree or 180-degree angle. The V1.1 model features a stabilizing pin on the male connector. Available in various colors, the adapters are designed in two variations to fit different power port orientations, with specific measurements for each model type.

The 90-degree adapter is 'L' shaped, while the 180-degree adapter is 'U' shaped. Consumers are urged to stop using these adapters immediately, as the male connector can loosen, overheat, and potentially melt into the GPU, leading to serious risks.
recalls.Title: CableMod GPU Angled Adapters Recalled for Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach CableMod at 888-203-3605 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email support@cablemod.com. More information is available online at https://cablemod.com/adapterrecall/ or by visiting https://cablemod.com and clicking on the 'Voluntary Safety Recall' banner.

Recalled 90-degree V1.0 Angled Adapter - Variant B
Recalled 180-degree V1.0 Angled Adapter - Variant A
Recalled 180-degree V1.0 Angled Adapter - Variant B
Recalled 90-degree V1.1 Angled Adapter - Variant A
Recalled 90-degree V1.1 Angled Adapter - Variant B
Recalled 180-degree V1.1 Angled Adapter - Variant A
Recalled 180-degree V1.1 Angled Adapter - Variant B

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 272 reports of the adapters becoming loose, overheating and melting into the GPU, with at least $74,500 in property damage claims in the United States. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at small independent stores nationwide and online at vprbrands.com from September 2022 to November 2023 for approximately $18.

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire and burns due to overheating connectors

Remedies.Name: Stop using the adapters, contact CableMod for safe removal instructions, and receive a full refund or $60 store credit.

recalls.metaDescription: CableMod recalls GPU Angled Adapters due to fire hazards. Contact for refunds and safe removal instructions.
